name: Native Browser Integrations
description: Web Share, Clipboard, Screen Wake Lock, and Fullscreen: four native browser APIs that bring a web app closer to an installed-app experience, with zero external dependencies.
longDescription: PWAs usually lose to native apps exactly where OS integration matters: no native share sheet, no control over screen dimming, no real immersive mode. This demo implements the four Web APIs that close each of those gaps using nothing but what the browser already ships — the Web Share API to open the OS's native share sheet, the Clipboard API for programmatic copy/paste, the Screen Wake Lock API to keep the screen active during use, and the Fullscreen API to strip away the browser chrome for an immersive view. None of them depend on an external library — it's the web platform alone reaching into the operating system.
tags: Web Share API, Clipboard API, Screen Wake Lock, Fullscreen API
architecture: Direct integration with native browser APIs, no external dependencies
technologies: Web Share API, Clipboard API, Screen Wake Lock API, Fullscreen API
concepts: Web Platform APIs, OS integration via the browser, native-like UX without installing an app
webShareDesc1: The Web Share API is a browser API that lets web apps use the device's native share sheet.
webShareDesc2: In practice, it lets a site or PWA share text, links, files, and other content directly with the apps installed on the user's phone or computer.
webShareUseCasesItem4: plug the app into the operating system's native share menu.
webShareExampleNote: A common example is the "Share" button found in many PWAs and modern sites: clicking it opens the device's native sheet, letting the user send the content to WhatsApp, Telegram, email, Discord, or any other installed app.
webShareValueNote: This is exactly the kind of integration that helps a web app behave like an installed native app, with no third-party SDK involved.
webShareHowToUseIntro: Click the button below to share this page using your device's native share sheet:
webShareUnsupportedMessage: The Web Share API isn't supported on this browser or platform.
webShareMethod1: navigator.canShare(data) — checks whether the data can be shared before invoking the share sheet
webShareMethod2: navigator.share(data) — opens the operating system's native share sheet
clipboardDesc1: The Clipboard API is a browser API that lets web apps read from and write to the user's clipboard.
clipboardDesc2: In practice, it lets a site or PWA copy or paste content programmatically — the same thing as the traditional Ctrl+C and Ctrl+V.
clipboardUseCasesItem3: copy links, commands, or code snippets;
clipboardUseCasesItem4: manipulate images and other data types.
clipboardExampleNote: A very common example is the "Copy" button next to Pix keys, terminal commands, or share links.
clipboardValueNote: For security and privacy reasons, the browser normally requires explicit user interaction — or a permission grant — before releasing clipboard access.
clipboardHowToUseIntro: Write some text in the first box below, copy it with the "Copy" button, and paste it into the second box with the "Paste" button.
clipboardUnavailableMessage: Couldn't access the clipboard right now — the API isn't available or the browser denied the request.
clipboardMethod1: navigator.clipboard.writeText() — writes text to the system clipboard
clipboardMethod2: navigator.clipboard.readText() — reads the text currently stored in the system clipboard
fullScreenDesc1: The Fullscreen API is a browser API that lets an element or the entire page be displayed in fullscreen mode.
fullScreenDesc2: In practice, it strips away the browser's own interface — tabs, address bar, menus — for a more immersive experience.
fullScreenUseCasesItem4: present dashboards and panels free of distractions;
fullScreenUseCasesItem5: bring the web experience closer to a native app's.
fullScreenExampleNote: A very common example is the fullscreen button found in video players, presentations, and streaming platforms.
fullScreenValueNote: For security and UX reasons, the browser normally requires an explicit interaction — like a click — before allowing fullscreen mode.
fullScreenHowToUseIntro: Click "Enter fullscreen" and watch the app take over the entire screen. To leave, click "Exit fullscreen".
fullScreenUnavailableMessage: The Fullscreen API isn't supported on this browser (Safari on iPhone, for example).
fullScreenMethod1: element.requestFullscreen() — puts a page element into fullscreen mode, hiding part of the browser's interface
fullScreenMethod2: document.exitFullscreen() — leaves fullscreen mode and returns to the page's normal view
wakeLockDesc1: The Screen Wake Lock API is a browser API that prevents the device's screen from automatically dimming or turning off while the app is in use.
wakeLockDesc2: In practice, it keeps the screen active even after long periods without user interaction — it works on both mobile devices and desktops/laptops, as long as the browser supports it.
wakeLockExampleNote: For example: if the system is set to turn off the screen after 5 minutes of inactivity, an app using Wake Lock can keep the screen on past that point while it's active.
wakeLockUseCasesIntro: This is especially useful in apps such as:
wakeLockWithoutApiNote: Without this API, the operating system would turn off the screen after a few minutes of inactivity, even with the app actively in use.
wakeLockCaveatNote: The browser and the operating system still control power management: in battery saver modes, or when the tab loses focus, the Wake Lock can be released automatically.
wakeLockPwaNote: In PWAs, the Screen Wake Lock API helps deliver an experience closer to a native app, especially in apps that need to stay visible continuously.
wakeLockHowToUseIntro: Check your device's configured screen timeout, then click the button below and watch that timeout be exceeded while the screen stays on:
wakeLockUnavailableMessage: Couldn't keep the screen on right now — the API isn't available or the browser denied the request.
wakeLockMethod1: navigator.wakeLock.request("screen") — requests the lock that keeps the screen always active
